items.headline: Summary
items.text: The world is on fire, oil is at $111, and AI just got a 1-million-token brain. Pick which one you care about more. Both will reshape how you work this week.

items.headline: AI
items.text: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.4 with a 1.05 million token context window and the ability to autonomously execute multi-step workflows. That is not an upgrade. That is a category shift. At the same time, Google Stitch dropped free and Figma stock fell 12% on day one. The pattern is clear: AI is making professional tools free. Finance reporting is next.

items.headline: World
items.text: Day 23 of the US-Israel-Iran war. Iran struck Arad and Dimona, injuring 100+. Trump threatened to obliterate Iran's power plants. Then he backed off for 5 days, citing "productive conversations." The Strait of Hormuz is still disrupted. IEA says this is the largest oil supply shock in history. Cuba went dark for the third time this month. Sudan lost 64 civilians in a hospital strike.

items.headline: Markets
items.text: Everything is red. S&P 500 down 1.51%. NASDAQ down 1.29%. BTC at $68k, ETH at $2,062. The one thing going up: oil. Brent crude at $111, up over 50% this month alone. Hormuz flows dropped from 20 million barrels per day to nearly zero. Goldman Sachs says triple-digit oil could stick for years. Energy sector is the only hedge that's working right now.

items.headline: Wild Card
items.text: Chinese AI is not losing. Five Chinese models dropped this month. MiniMax M2.5 now rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at a fraction of the cost. Kimi K2.5 runs on Cloudflare's edge. The West is spending billions on AI infrastructure. China is shipping cheaper, faster models. The cost war is on. And the winner is every developer who does not care which flag is on the model card.

items.headline: GPT-5.4: 1.05M Tokens, Autonomous Execution, 75% on OSWorld-V
items.text: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 sets a new commercial benchmark with a 1.05-million-token context window. The model scores 75% on OSWorld-V, just above the human baseline of 72.4%, and can autonomously execute multi-step workflows across software environments. Mini version now rolling out to Free and Go users. OpenAI has surpassed $25B in annualized revenue and is eyeing a late-2026 IPO.

items.headline: Gemini 3.1 Pro: 77.1% ARC-AGI-2, 1M Context, Full Multimodal
items.text: Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.1 Pro scores 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 with a 1M-token context window and multimodal reasoning across text, images, audio, video, and code. Flash-Lite variant offers 2.5x faster responses at just $0.25 per million input tokens, making it the most cost-effective frontier model available.

items.headline: Google Stitch: Free AI Design Tool Crashes Figma 12%
items.text: Google Stitch builds working, high-fidelity reports and interfaces from plain-language prompts. No wireframes, no design background needed. It critiques its own work in real time, responds to voice, and deploys in one click. The day it launched, Figma dropped 12%. Finance reporting, legal documents, and board materials are all in the crosshairs next.

items.headline: Five Chinese Models Drop: MiniMax M2.5 Rivals Claude Opus 4.6
items.text: Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance, and MiniMax all launched models in March. MiniMax's M2.5 rivals Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 at a significantly lower cost. Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 is now available in Cloudflare Workers AI for edge inference, no server management required. Chinese models are winning the cost war.

items.headline: Nemotron 3 Super: 120B Parameters, 12B Active, Built for Agents
items.text: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Super is a 120B-total-parameter hybrid Mixture-of-Experts model with only 12B active parameters per forward pass. Announced at GTC on March 11, it's designed specifically for complex multi-agent enterprise applications where inference efficiency matters as much as raw capability.

items.headline: Anthropic Rolls Out Memory for All Claude Users
items.text: Anthropic's persistent memory feature is now available to all Claude users. The assistant retains context, preferences, and project history across conversations. Combined with Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 (released in February), Anthropic now offers a 1-million-token context window in beta with improved coding capabilities. Over 30 OpenAI and Google employees publicly backed Anthropic's DOD lawsuit.

items.headline: MIT AI Model Slashes Drug Development Costs by Billions
items.text: MIT's protein-based drug design model predicts how synthetic proteins fold and interact with molecular targets, cutting pharmaceutical R&D costs by billions of dollars. The model accelerates treatment development for cancer, autoimmune diseases, and rare genetic disorders. Medical AI matured from research curiosity to pipeline reality this month.

items.headline: Kimi K2.5 on Cloudflare Edge: Frontier AI Without Servers
items.text: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 is now available in the Cloudflare Workers AI ecosystem, enabling developers to run a frontier-level AI model directly on edge computing infrastructure. No servers to manage, no GPU clusters to provision. This signals the next phase: frontier-grade AI as a commodity infrastructure layer.

items.headline: Iran-US-Israel War Day 23: Hormuz Ultimatum, Trump Pauses Strikes
items.text: Iran struck the Israeli cities of Arad and Dimona (near Israel's nuclear research center), injuring 100+ people. Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on Iran's largest power plant. Iran warned it would "completely close" Hormuz if power plants are struck. Trump then halted all US energy infrastructure strikes for five days, citing productive diplomatic conversations. Nothing is resolved.

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz: IEA Calls It the Worst Supply Shock in History
items.text: The International Energy Agency warns the Hormuz disruption is the largest supply shock in the history of the global oil market. Crude and oil product flows through the Strait have fallen from around 20 million barrels per day before the war to near zero currently. Goldman Sachs says triple-digit oil prices could persist for years if the conflict is not resolved.

items.headline: Ukraine: Low-Tech Overhead Netting Deployed Against Russian FPV Drones
items.text: Ukrainian towns are installing overhead netting as a low-cost defense against Russian FPV (first-person view) drones — the latest generation of cheap, precise, lethal weapons deployed by Russian forces. Cities along the advancing eastern front line are preparing infrastructure for sustained urban combat. Russia's full-scale invasion is now in its fifth year.

items.headline: Cuba Goes Dark for Third Time in March as Grid Collapses Again
items.text: Cuba's power grid collapsed for the third time in March, leaving the entire country without electricity. The communist government is battling a combination of decaying Soviet-era infrastructure, lack of investment, and a US oil blockade that has gutted energy generation capacity. The recurring blackouts signal a systemic collapse, not a temporary failure.

items.text: Forge enterprise platform launched at NVIDIA GTC, enabling "build-your-own-AI" for enterprises. On track to surpass $1B annual recurring revenue this year. Mistral's bet: enterprises want full control over their AI stack, not dependence on OpenAI or Google.

items.headline: Meta AI
items.text: Llama deployments continue expanding across enterprise. Meta's open-source strategy keeps Llama in every stack where developers want to avoid vendor lock-in. No major model launch this week but Meta's infrastructure bets (custom silicon, data center expansion) keep it competitive on cost.

items.headline: China AI
items.text: Five models launched this month from Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance, and MiniMax. MiniMax M2.5 rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at a fraction of the cost. Kimi K2.5 runs on Cloudflare's edge. Chinese AI is winning on price and shipping speed. The West's infrastructure spending advantage may not translate to model quality lead for long.

items.headline: Don't Let AI Become Your Exoskeleton
items.text: GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 can do your thinking for you. That is exactly the problem. Use AI to accelerate your thinking, not replace it. Feed your analysis to the model. Don't ask the model for your analysis. The exoskeleton effect is real: professionals who outsource judgment to AI lose the muscle over time. Keep the hard thinking in your head. Use AI for execution and research.

items.headline: Build Agentic Workflows Without Code
items.text: n8n, Make.com, and Zapier Central all let you build AI agents visually. No code required. A research agent that runs daily costs $5-20/month in API fees. The 2026 architecture that matters most: a RAG-grounded agent with a vector database backend (Pinecone), a frontier model for reasoning (GPT-5.4 or Claude), and a workflow layer for orchestration (n8n). Build this and you have an employee that works 24/7 for $25/month.

items.headline: Turn Napkin Numbers into CFO Reports with Google Stitch
items.text: Google Stitch is free. Go to stitch.google.com, upload a photo of your hand-drawn numbers or paste raw data, and use this prompt template: "Build an executive financial report. Lead with a single headline that states the conclusion. Show three key metrics with context. Include a 12-month trend chart where green = over plan, red = under. Add three driver callouts explaining the story." You will get a board-ready report in under 60 seconds.

items.headline: GPT-5.4 is Now the Best Coding Model for Long Context
items.text: 1.05 million tokens means GPT-5.4 can read your entire codebase in one pass. For refactoring, debugging across files, and architecture reviews, this is the new standard. Use it with a strong system prompt: "You are a senior engineer reviewing this codebase. Identify the three highest-risk areas. For each, explain the risk, the impact, and the fix." You get a full engineering audit in seconds.

items.headline: Use Pinecone + n8n for Persistent AI Memory
items.text: Anthropic added memory to Claude. But if you want cross-platform, cross-model AI memory, build it yourself. Store all your conversations, documents, and work outputs as vector embeddings in Pinecone. Connect it to n8n. Every time you start a new AI conversation, retrieve the top 5 most relevant memories first. This gives you context persistence across any model, any tool, any platform.

items.headline: Deploy Frontier AI on Cloudflare Workers: Zero Servers
items.text: Kimi K2.5 is now available in Cloudflare Workers AI. You can call a frontier-level AI model in a Cloudflare Worker with three lines of code. No GPU servers, no scaling headaches, no cold starts. This is the cheapest way to add AI to any web application. Response times are under 200ms globally. Try it: build a one-page app that summarizes any URL you paste into it. Cost: near zero.

The frontier model war just became a platform war. Pick your stack carefully.

items.text: Iran fired missiles near Israel's Dimona nuclear center. That is a first. 100+ injured, and Trump gave a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Over 5,000 vessels are stranded. Iran responded by threatening full closure. Diego Garcia, a US-UK base in the Indian Ocean, also took fire. This is not contained anymore.

items.text: Everything is red. S&P down 1.51%. NASDAQ down 2.01%. Bitcoin dropped 2.77% to $68,616. Brent crude surged past $106 on Hormuz fears. Goldman says triple-digit oil could last years if the strait stays closed. The sell-off is energy-driven, not tech-driven. Watch oil, not earnings.

items.text: The Pentagon just made Palantir's Maven a permanent program of record. That means long-term military AI funding across every branch. AI in defense went from experimental to infrastructure overnight. Meanwhile, you can now run DeepSeek and Llama on your phone with zero internet. Private AI is real. The gap between what governments build and what individuals can run is shrinking fast.

items.headline: DoorDash Tasks — 8M Drivers Become AI Data Collectors
items.text: DoorDash launched Tasks, paying its 8 million Dashers to photograph menus, check shelves, and record walkthroughs for real-time business intelligence. The delivery workforce becomes the world's largest AI data collection pipeline — a novel crowdsourced approach to training AI on real-world physical environments.

items.headline: MiniMax M2.5 Rivals Claude Opus at Lower Cost
items.text: Chinese lab MiniMax released M2.5, reportedly rivaling Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 at significantly lower cost. This continues the pattern of Chinese models delivering frontier-level performance at a fraction of Western prices, pressuring the economics of the entire AI industry.

items.headline: Cursor Composer 2 — Enhanced AI Coding
items.text: Cursor released Composer 2 with enhanced AI coding capabilities, delivering frontier-level code generation at improved cost-performance ratios. The upgrade cements Cursor's position as the dominant AI-native IDE for professional developers and pressures competing coding tools.

items.text: MIT researchers developed a new AI model for protein-based drug design that could accelerate pharmaceutical R&D pipelines. The model enables more precise targeting of disease mechanisms through computational protein engineering, potentially reducing the time and cost of bringing new therapeutics to clinical trials.

items.headline: Iran War Week 4: Hormuz Blocked, Oil Above $110
items.text: The US-Israel conflict with Iran enters week four. The Strait of Hormuz is blocked with 3,000+ vessels stranded and oil above $110/barrel. Trump threatens to "hit and obliterate" Iranian power plants while the humanitarian toll mounts. No clear off-ramp is in sight as both sides escalate.

items.text: The White House is sending mixed signals — Trump publicly suggested winding down Iran operations while simultaneously approving additional Marine deployments. The contradiction underscores the lack of a coherent exit strategy as the conflict enters its fourth week with escalating costs.

items.text: Iran launched ballistic missiles at a US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean, marking a significant escalation in direct military confrontation. The strike represents one of the most direct attacks on Western military infrastructure since the conflict began, raising fears of further escalation.

items.text: Over 1,000 people have been killed in Lebanon since March 2, including 111 children. The humanitarian crisis deepens as the regional conflict expands beyond its initial scope, with civilian casualties mounting and international calls for a ceasefire going unanswered.

items.text: A federal jury found Elon Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors before his $44B acquisition. One of the highest-profile securities fraud findings in Silicon Valley history, with potentially significant financial implications and questions about accountability for market manipulation.

items.headline: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director, Dies at 81
items.text: Robert Mueller, who served as FBI Director and later as Special Counsel investigating Russian interference in the 2016 election, has died at age 81. A defining figure in American law enforcement and justice, Mueller's career spanned decades of public service at the highest levels.

items.headline: The Exoskeleton Effect
items.text: AI amplifies capability but over-reliance atrophies judgment. Use AI as a collaborator, not a replacement. Maintain critical thinking skills alongside AI tools to avoid dependency and preserve decision-making quality.

items.headline: No-Code Agentic Workflows
items.text: Gumloop enables no-code agentic workflows. n8n provides open-source automation. Claude Code + MCP servers let you build sophisticated agents without traditional coding — lowering the barrier to production-grade AI automation.

items.headline: Desktop AI & On-Device Inference
items.text: NVIDIA DGX Station brings data center AI to the desktop. Apple Private Cloud Compute runs Gemini models with strict privacy guarantees. On-device inference is becoming practical as smaller MoE models reduce compute requirements.

items.headline: Sketch-to-Production Design
items.text: Google Stitch turns sketches into production designs for free. Generative 3D and video tools are maturing rapidly. AI-assisted design is now accessible to non-designers, democratizing visual communication and reporting.

items.text: Copilot Agent Mode orchestrates cross-app tasks autonomously within Microsoft 365. Match workflow architecture to the specific use case rather than chasing maximum autonomy — the right level of AI assistance depends on the task.

items.text: Claude Opus 4.6 is the current best coding model with 1M context. Cursor Composer 2 and Claude Code Channels expand AI coding accessibility. Together they represent the frontier of AI-assisted software development.

entries.title: The Open-Weight Flood: Why 78% of March's AI Models Were Free

items.headline: The Open-Weight Flood: Why 78% of March's AI Models Were Free
items.text: Seven out of nine major text models released in March 2026 were free to download. Read that again. 78%. Not demos. Not waitlists. Not "contact sales." Full-weight models you can run on your own hardware, fine-tune for your own use case, and deploy without asking permission. The proof is in the release logs. The promise is simple: the economics of AI just flipped. The plan for this article is to show you exactly what that means, what to ignore, and what to build.

items.headline: The Commoditization Curve
items.text: Every technology follows the same arc. It starts expensive and rare. Then it gets cheap and abundant. Then the value migrates somewhere else entirely. I call this the Commoditization Curve, and AI models just hit the steep part. Think of it in three layers: Layer 1: The Model. This is where the industry spent 2023 and 2024 competing. Who has the best weights? Who tops the leaderboard? That era is ending. When Alibaba releases Qwen3.5 in eight variants from 0.8B to 397B parameters under Apache 2.

items.headline: The Strategic Logic
items.text: Each open release tells a different story, but they share one thesis: giving away the model creates asymmetric advantage somewhere else. Alibaba's eight-variant blitz is a volume play. Qwen3.5 covers every deployment scenario from mobile (0.8B) to data center (397B). The goal is not model revenue. It is ecosystem capture. Every developer who builds on Qwen is one more node in Alibaba's cloud flywheel. The model is free. The compute to run it is not. NVIDIA's Nemotron giveaway follows the same lo

items.headline: The Counterargument Is Real
items.text: I want to be honest about the limits. Proprietary models still lead open-weight alternatives by roughly 9 benchmark points on complex reasoning tasks, according to March 2026 evaluations (WhatLLM). That gap justifies 10 to 20 times higher token costs for premium use cases. If you are building an AI-powered legal review system or a medical diagnostic tool, those 9 points matter. Sam Altman described AI as "a metered utility like electricity" (UniladTech, March 2026). He is positioning GPT-5.4 not

items.headline: The Enterprise Knife Fight
items.text: Meanwhile, the proprietary players are not sitting still. OpenAI is undercutting Anthropic on private equity deals (TransformerNews). xAI is wooing enterprises with on-site engineers. The competition for the 20% of use cases that justify premium pricing is getting vicious. This is what a commoditizing market looks like. The middle disappears. You either compete on cost (open-weight) or on premium service (proprietary with white-glove support). There is no comfortable position between.

Project this forward five years. If open-weight models close that 9-point reasoning gap (and the pace suggests they will within 18 to 24 months), the value of proprietary weights drops to near zero. What remains valuable is three things: inference infrastructure, safety alignment, and regulatory relationships. This might mean the AI industry in 2031 looks more like Linux than like Windows.
